name: Alpha Vantage API
what_it_does: Free and premium REST API for stock prices, forex rates, cryptocurrency data, technical indicators, and company fundamentals, with no official SDK.
use_cases: Simple stock price lookups for low-frequency financial agents or prototypes
Technical indicator computation (RSI, MACD, SMA, EMA) without implementing the math
Fundamental data access — earnings, balance sheet, income statement — for equity analysis
Forex exchange rate lookups for currency conversion in automated pipelines
Economic indicator data (CPI, GDP, unemployment) for macro-aware agents
not_for: Any agent making more than 25 API calls per day on the free tier
Real-time or low-latency trading (free data is 15-20 minutes delayed)
Options chain or Level 2 order book data
Production agents — rate limit errors are invisible at the HTTP level
best_when: You are prototyping a financial agent with minimal budget and need a broad variety of indicators and data types without writing the math yourself.
avoid_when: Your agent will call the API more than a few dozen times per day on the free tier, or when you need reliable error signaling — the API returns HTTP 200 for rate limit errors.

auth.notes: API key passed as apikey query parameter. No header-based auth option. Free key available immediately with email only. Key is visible in URLs and server logs — a security concern for production deployments.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 25 requests/day; 15-20 minute data delay; limited to core stock, forex, and crypto endpoints
pricing.paid_tiers: Premium from $50/month (75 req/min, real-time data); $120/month (600 req/min, fundamentals); higher tiers available
pricing.notes: Free tier has become increasingly restrictive over time — the 25 req/day daily cap was tightened from earlier 5/minute limits. Any agent doing real work needs a paid plan. Premium pricing is moderate but not cheap relative to alternatives like Polygon.io.

performance.rate_limits: Free: 25 requests/day; Premium: 75-1200 requests/minute depending on tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CRITICAL flaw: the API returns HTTP 200 for all responses including rate limit errors, expired keys, and invalid parameters. Errors appear as 'Note' or 'Information' string fields in the JSON body. Agents that check HTTP status codes will silently receive error messages instead of data and may not detect failure.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are read-only GET requests — idempotent by nature.
known_agent_gotchas: CRITICAL: Rate limit and error responses return HTTP 200 — agents must parse the response body for 'Note' or 'Information' fields to detect failure
Free tier cap of 25 requests/day makes it nearly unusable for any real agent workflow
No official SDK — agents must implement their own HTTP client and response parsing
API key is passed as a query parameter, exposing it in logs and browser history
Response format (CSV vs JSON) varies by endpoint and must be specified explicitly
Some older endpoints have been deprecated without a clear migration path or changelog
Data delay on free tier (15-20 min) is not signaled in response metadata — agents cannot detect staleness

security_score_components.security_notes: API key exposed as query parameter is a significant security issue for production. No scopes, no key rotation tooling, no IP allowlisting on free/lower tiers.
